About this app

One platform, three ways to grow your local business: find customers (B2C), source from suppliers & sell to other shops (B2B), and hire local talent (Jobs). Built for the high streets of Kerala — locality to state-wide reach.

Placeholder install screen. The working product is the PWA (Web / App tabs) — this shows how a Play Store listing would present it.
★ Kodungalloor's high street, online

Member view · source & sell within the network

B2B procurement network

Shops source from each other and from wholesalers, distributors & manufacturers. You find them here and deal directly — call, WhatsApp or visit. BNC surfaces who's available; the trade stays between you.

Pricing · pay by reach

Plans for shops & suppliers

Shops pay a low yearly fee scaled by how wide they're seen. Suppliers — wholesalers, distributors, manufacturers — pay more, on monthly or yearly plans, because they reach hundreds of shops at once.

Starter

₹199 / yr
~₹17 / month
  • Listing + Google search
  • 1 category · 2 photos
  • Simple digital card
  • 5 job posts / yr
  • Analytics · featured
Reach: Locality

Silver

₹499 / yr
~₹42 / month
  • Banner + 5 photos
  • 3 categories · grouping
  • Map · hours · description
  • 15 job posts · basic analytics
  • 1 free featured week
Reach: Locality + nearby
Popular

Gold

₹999 / yr
~₹83 / month
  • 10 photos · 5 categories
  • Auto-leads inbox
  • Unlimited job posts
  • Customer reviews
  • 2 featured weeks / yr
Reach: Constituency

Platinum

₹1,499 / yr
~₹125 / month
  • 15 photos · 7 categories
  • Promo video slot
  • Constituency priority rank
  • Competitor benchmarking
  • 1 featured month / yr
Reach: Constituency priority

Diamond

₹2,499 / yr
~₹208 / month
  • Unlimited photos · categories
  • District priority rank
  • 2 locations · RFQ tools
  • Quarterly business report
  • 2 featured months / yr
Reach: District

Ruby

₹2,999 / yr
~₹250 / month
  • Everything in Diamond
  • Up to 5 locations
  • State-wide priority rank
  • POS / API access (beta)
  • Account manager check-in
Reach: State-wide

Locations111125
Every shop is visible in its locality regardless of tier — higher plans rank first and reach wider, never hide the rest. Reviews can lift a smaller shop above a bigger one, keeping competition healthy.

Short-term · priority display · for launches & seasonal pushes

Launch

1 Month

₹2,500 / month
  • Full priority display
  • "Featured this month" badge
  • 1 locality or 2–3 nearby
  • Direct messaging to all shops
  • Daily performance analytics
Best for a single product launch

2 Months

₹4,500 / 2 mo
₹2,250 / month
  • Everything in 1-month
  • 5–6 areas coverage
  • 1 virtual launch event
Better value than monthly

3 Months

₹6,500 / 3 mo
₹2,167 / month
  • Everything in 2-month
  • Full constituency coverage
  • Monthly email campaign
Seasonal / quarterly promotions

Long-term · annual presence · steady reach all year

Starter

₹3,000 / yr
₹250 / month
  • 1 locality
  • 20 products / offers
  • Direct messaging to shops
  • Monthly analytics
  • Featured (add-on)
New / niche suppliers
Popular

Growth

₹6,000 / yr
₹500 / month
  • 2–3 localities
  • Unlimited products
  • 1 featured month included
  • 2 email campaigns / yr
  • 1 virtual launch / yr
Established distributors

Established

₹9,000 / yr
₹750 / month
  • Full constituency
  • 2 featured months included
  • 4 email campaigns / yr
  • 2 virtual launches / yr
  • Priority support
Large distributors

Premium

₹12,000 / yr
₹1,000 / month
  • Multi-constituency / district
  • 3 featured months included
  • Unlimited email campaigns
  • Quarterly trade events
  • Dedicated account manager
State suppliers · franchises
Suppliers carry the higher price because the value is clear — one plan reaches every subscribed shop in the chosen area. Add-ons (featured boost ₹2,500/mo, email broadcast ₹1,500, virtual launch ₹2,000) layer on top of any plan.

Coordination structure

Service-based geography model — Coordinating Partners support Grassroots Partners, who onboard & assist vendors.

Super-adminEverQPlatform · policy · approvals
CPDistrict Coordinating PartnerTrains & supports grassroots partners
GLPGrassroots Level PartnerOnboards vendors locality by locality

🍌 Avinipully Vegetables
For the founders — not part of the product

Reading of the concept

The product tabs are a faithful build of the model as agreed: three modes (B2C, B2B, Jobs) in one membership, low shop pricing, suppliers paying for reach. Here's where EverQ's judgement sits on top.

Three modes, one membership Keep

B2C visibility, B2B sourcing and a jobs board in a single subscription is what makes ₹199–₹2,999 feel like a bargain. A shop pays once and gets customers, suppliers and hiring. That bundle is the reason to subscribe — none of the three alone would justify it.

Shops cheap, suppliers carry the revenue Keep

Pricing shops at ₹199–₹2,999/yr wins density; charging suppliers ₹2,500/mo or ₹3,000–₹12,000/yr is where the money is, because one supplier plan reaches every shop in an area. The ROI is obvious to a distributor and trivial to a shop. This cross-subsidy is the engine of the model.

B2B trades happen off-platform Refine

By design BNC surfaces suppliers and lets shops deal directly (call/WhatsApp) — this dodges the inventory-sync and fulfilment problems that sank the earlier marketplace. The trade-off: BNC can't see transaction value, so it can't charge commission. Fine for now — but capturing even lightweight order logging later would unlock analytics and a future fee tier without rebuilding logistics.

Healthy competition, nobody buried Keep

Higher tiers rank first and reach wider, but every shop stays visible in its locality and reviews can lift a smaller shop. This is the right balance: it gives a real reason to upgrade without making the free/cheap tiers pointless — which is what keeps the directory full.

Partner model stays clean Keep

CP supports GLP; GLP onboards vendors. Partners earn from coordinating real listings in their geography, never from recruiting other partners. That single line keeps this clear of the chit/MLM zone — worth protecting in the Terms.

Consent, approvals & data law Watch

Now holding customer, merchant and job-seeker data — including résumé-style profiles. The listing-consent flow and Terms should be set for India's DPDP regime and checked by a legal professional, especially the jobs/personal-data side. This is product guidance, not legal advice.

BNC Be A Winner — the engagement flywheel Keep

QR poster → free join → member number → sponsored draws → winner collects in person at the sponsor shop → footfall + landing-page fame → more shops sponsor → denser directory → suppliers pay more. The "collect at the shop" rule is the linchpin: it turns a prize draw into real footfall and keeps BNC out of prize logistics, money and delivery entirely — the same discipline that protects against the old marketplace's failure.

Shop-attributed QR Keep

Every poster carries the shop's own code, so each join is credited to the shop that displayed it. The poster stops being charity — shops compete to recruit customers, and you get a clean growth-attribution trail for partner payouts and "top recruiter" recognition.

Draws, lottery rules & data law Watch

Entry must stay always free and sponsor-funded (never pay-to-enter), draws transparent and recorded, and member data handled under India's DPDP regime. Prize draws can touch Kerala's lottery/gaming rules — have a legal professional confirm the structure and consent flow before launch. Product guidance, not legal advice.

Pricing now reflects your figures: shops ₹199–₹2,999/yr, suppliers ₹2,500/mo short-term and ₹3,000–₹12,000/yr long-term. The taxonomy, tiers and copy are all editable in one file. This mockup runs fully offline for the meeting.
